refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral and specifies what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There is no conjunction or multiple obligations to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to control packet loss
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between retransmission logic and teleoperation control timing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality control within the protocol design, supporting independence from other system functions. Adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms can be modularized to minimize coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters; unexpected network degradation may impact performance
Adaptive error correction may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Complexity of protocol may increase system resource usage
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency constraints alongside packet loss to ensure teleoperation responsiveness is maintained under the selected DP.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network communication stack and protocol implementations responsible for managing data transmission, including error correction and retransmission mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network layer for sending and receiving packets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send status and performance data related to communication quality and error correction metrics.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol logically belongs here; minimal diffusion since error correction is core to network communication.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol; would cause high diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Teleoperation depends on communication but protocol implementation here would mix concerns and increase diffusion.
Safety Services may monitor communication but should not implement protocol; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication functions and can encapsulate adaptive error correction and retransmission logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
If protocol parameters need to be adjusted by other modules, interface complexity may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, fulfilling the functional requirement of supporting teleoperation with required communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
If protocol parameters need to be adjusted by other modules, interface complexity may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive for containing the communication protocol DP, with a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2). The protocol implementation is logically centralized here, minimizing diffusion risk and supporting clear ownership and encapsulation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and validating that packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as this module encompasses the network communication stack responsible for error correction and retransmission mechanisms required to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document interfaces between NGHE Solution and lower-level hardware/network layers to reduce dependency risks.
Define configuration interfaces or parameter adjustment mechanisms if other modules need to influence protocol behavior, to manage interface complexity and maintain modularity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a well-defined design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module encapsulates the communication protocol effectively with low diffusion risk and clear ownership. Verification feasibility is established, and the allocation is appropriate. Although some interface clarifications and parameter adjustment mechanisms are recommended, these do not necessitate further functional decomposition at this stage, especially given the high safety criticality which favors stability and clarity over unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: Clarify and document interfaces between NGHE Solution and lower-level hardware/network layers to reduce dependency risks, and define configuration interfaces or parameter adjustment mechanisms to manage interface complexity and maintain modularity.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR as specified.
The NGHE Solution module can be verified through telemetry monitoring under agreed network conditions.
Interface clarifications and parameter adjustments can be handled within the current IM without further decomposition.
open_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
Interface complexity may increase if protocol parameters need adjustment by other modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interfaces between NGHE Solution and hardware/network layers.
Specification of configuration interfaces for protocol parameter adjustments.
Verification results confirming packet loss remains within customer-defined limits under test conditions.
